title: Isoetes andicola
taxonomy: Isoetes andicola is classified within the family Isoetaceae, which is a group of aquatic plants commonly known as quillworts. The Isoetes genus is part of the order Isoetales, and the species I. andicola is native to South America. Isoetes andicola is further classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae, specifically the division Lycopodiophyta. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other lycopod plants. As a member of the Isoetaceae family, I. andicola exhibits adaptations suited to its aquatic environment.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Isoetes andicola include sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties.
